name: Momentum Win
description: Back set 1 winner after a decisive non-tiebreak set — market often over-corrects post-set

name: Underdog Confirmed
description: Pre-match underdog (2.5+) wins set 1 convincingly — strongest documented edge in tennis betting

name: Dominant Favourite
description: Heavy favourite (≤1.75) wins set 1 dominantly (28%+ price move) — confirmation play at short odds

name: Tiebreak Winner
description: Set 1 decided by tiebreak — winner holds psychological momentum into set 2

name: Tiebreak Loser Value
description: After a tiebreak, back the LOSER — match is essentially level so inflated odds represent value

name: Dominant Set Win
description: Largest price moves (>30%) — dominant first set performance, strongest continuation signal

name: [LOSING] Back Any Set Winner
description: LOSING BASELINE — back any set 1 winner with no filters. Too broad: ~52% win rate at average 1.6 odds needs 62.5% to break even. Included as a comparison baseline.

name: [LOSING] Back Set 1 Loser
description: LOSING BASELINE — back the set 1 loser regardless of context. Set 1 loser wins only ~33-35% of ATP matches; at 2.0 odds needs 50%+ to profit. Structural losing bet.

name: [LOSING] Favourite Confirmation
description: LOSING BASELINE — back any favourite (≤1.89 pre-match) after winning set 1. No edge: market already prices favourites correctly so no value remains after the set.
